req.title: Manager creates team
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a team through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers team creation via REST API with mandatory field validation and audit logging. I selected: (1) EP positive – a fully valid manager request to confirm happy-path creation and audit write; (2) NEG – missing mandatory 'name' field to confirm 400 validation error; (3) BVA – team name at exactly 1 character (minimum boundary) to confirm acceptance vs. rejection edge; (4) NEG/DT – unauthenticated request to confirm 401; (5) DT – authenticated but non-manager role to confirm 403. Together these cover the three required kinds (positive, negative, boundary) and all four error pathways in the requirement.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_team_valid_payload
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
No team named 'Alpha Squad' already exists
actions: POST /api/teams with valid JSON body containing all mandatory fields
Query audit log for the returned team id
expected: Response body contains a non-empty 'id' field
Audit record exists for the created team id
trace: R-WEB-009: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_creates_team_valid_payload(manager_session, audit_log_client, unique_team_name):
    payload = {
        "name": unique_team_name,
        "department": "Engineering",
        "manager_id": manager_session.user_id
    }
    resp = manager_session.post("/api/teams", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    team_id = body.get("id")
    assert team_id, "Response must contain a non-empty 'id'"
    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=team_id, action="CREATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record must be written on successful creation"

tests.name: test_create_team_missing_mandatory_name_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
actions: POST /api/teams with JSON body omitting the mandatory 'name' field
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing 'name'
trace: R-WEB-009: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_create_team_missing_mandatory_name_returns_400(manager_session):
    payload = {
        "department": "Engineering",
        "manager_id": manager_session.user_id
    }
    resp = manager_session.post("/api/teams", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    errors = str(body).lower()
    assert "name" in errors, "Error body must reference the missing 'name' field"

tests.name: test_create_team_name_minimum_length_one_char
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Minimum allowed team name length is 1 character
actions: POST /api/teams with team name of exactly 1 character
expected: Response body contains a non-empty 'id' field
trace: R-WEB-009: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-009: response 200 or 201 with entity id

tests.name: test_create_team_unauthenticated_returns_401
actions: POST /api/teams with a valid JSON body but no auth token

tests.name: test_create_team_non_manager_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'employee' (not manager)
actions: POST /api/teams with a valid JSON body using an employee-role session

tests.name: test_create_team_name_exceeds_maximum_length_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Maximum allowed team name length is 255 characters
actions: POST /api/teams with team name of 256 characters (one beyond maximum)
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing 'name'
trace: R-WEB-009: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_create_team_name_exceeds_maximum_length_returns_400(manager_session):
    oversized_name = "A" * 256
    payload = {
        "name": oversized_name,
        "department": "Engineering",
        "manager_id": manager_session.user_id
    }
    resp = manager_session.post("/api/teams", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 400, (
        f"256-char name should be rejected; got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    errors = str(body).lower()
    assert "name" in errors, "Error body must reference the offending 'name' field"
